title: Austin Robotaxi Fleet Hits 10 Unsupervised Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin unsupervised Robotaxi fleet reached 10 Model Y vehicles, the highest confirmed count to date. This reflects ongoing ramp-up in the primary Texas hub.
impact_reason: Demonstrates continued fleet growth in Austin to a record 10 vehicles, supporting steady unsupervised scaling velocity in the core operational area.

title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Two Unsupervised Vehicles Each
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla deployed two unsupervised vehicles in Dallas and two in Houston shortly after April 18 launch. Austin currently has 13 unsupervised vehicles active in the last 30 days.
impact_reason: Confirms small-scale unsupervised operations in new cities, providing incremental momentum and shared learning for Austin fleet expansion without direct Austin growth.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, with geofence maps for initial service areas and video evidence of no safety driver. This triples Texas hubs alongside Austin, aligning with H1 2026 seven-city rollout plans.
impact_reason: Expansion to two new Texas cities directly boosts regional scaling infrastructure and data collection for Austin fleet growth, though initial deployments are small.

title: Elon Musk Announces Austin Robotaxi Fleet to Double to ~60 Vehicles
summary: Elon Musk stated the Austin Robotaxi fleet, currently at 29 supervised vehicles per community tracker, will double to about 60 next month. This falls short of the Q3 2025 goal of 500 vehicles by year-end.
impact_reason: Doubling fleet size signals positive growth trajectory in Austin but remains modest and supervised, limiting unsupervised scaling velocity.

title: Tesla Discloses 14 Crashes in Austin Robotaxi Operations
summary: In a February filing, Tesla reported 14 crashes involving its Austin robotaxis since launch.
impact_reason: Disclosed crashes introduce mild safety scrutiny that could slightly slow regulatory approvals or public acceptance, indirectly dampening unsupervised fleet scaling velocity in Austin.
